Now, what distinguishes the target from the distractors?


The target starts with a filled column, unlike distractor 1 which begins empty. It also has two consecutive empty columns in the middle—columns 2 and 3—whereas distractor 2 breaks this pattern with a filled column between them. The target ends with two consecutive filled columns, creating a symmetric structure that neither distractor shares. (pattern: □ □ X X X)
- Distractor 2 has columns 1, 3, 4 filled (pattern: X □ X X □)

The target stands out because it's the only grid with a filled first column paired with two empty columns in the middle. This combination doesn't appear in either distractor—one starts empty on the left while the other breaks up its empty space differently.

content.text: Expression: The grid with the leftmost column filled and two consecutive empty columns in the middle positions.
